title: Drill string jar
abstract: A drilling jar includes an outer tubular housing having an inner bore. An inner tubular assembly is telescopically received within the housing. A valve assembly is disposed between the inner tubular assembly and the outer housing. The valve assembly disposed between first and second oil chambers, wherein in response to an axial force applied to the inner tubular assembly or the outer housing the valve assembly travels to meter fluid communication between the oil chambers. The inner tubular assembly includes a housing having longitudinal spline ridge cuts and a mandrel having spline ridge protrusions. The mandrel is disposed within the housing. A ridge protrusion on the mandrel having an angled surface to provide an oil flow channel when the protrusion engages with a ridge cut. A drilling jar including a mechanical jar module.
claims:
Claims 1. A drilling jar for subsurface disposal, comprising: an outer tubular housing having an inner bore; an inner tubular assembly telescopically received within the bore of outer tubular housing; a valve assembly comprising a first valve linked to a second valve, the assembly disposed in an annulus between the inner tubular assembly and the outer tubular housing; the valve assembly disposed in between a first oil chamber and a second oil chamber, wherein in response to an axial force applied to the inner tubular assembly or the outer tubular housing the valve assembly is configured to travel longitudinally within the annulus to meter fluid communication between the first oil chamber and the second oil chamber; the inner tubular assembly comprising a housing having longitudinal spline ridge cuts on an interior surface thereof; a mandrel having spline ridge protrusions on an exterior surface thereof; the mandrel disposed within the inner tubular assembly housing with the spline ridge protrusions matingly engaged with the housing spline ridge cuts; and at least one of the spline ridge protrusions on the mandrel having an angled surface to provide a flow channel for the oil when the spline ridge protrusion is matingly engaged with a housing spline ridge cut. 2. The drilling jar of claim 1 wherein the first oil chamber, the second oil chamber, and the flow channel defined by the spline ridge protrusion engaged with the housing spline ridge cut are linked to provide an internal fluid passageway to allow oil flow internally along the length of the jar. 3. The drilling jar of claim 2 wherein the valve assembly is configured to travel longitudinally within the annulus through a radially-narrowed section in the annulus. 4. The drilling jar of claim 3 wherein upon passage of the first valve or the second valve through the radially-narrowed annulus section oil flow between the first oil chamber and the second oil chamber is increased. 5. The drilling jar of claim 4 wherein a mandrel of the inner tubular assembly is configured to deliver an impact blow to an internal anvil when the oil flow between the first oil chamber and the second oil chamber is increased. 6. The drilling jar of claim 5 wherein the mandrel having the spline ridge protrusions is configured to telescopically slide within the inner tubular assembly housing, with the oil flow facilitated internally along the housing via the flow channel. 7. The drilling jar of claim 1 further comprising a piston assembly to accommodate thermal expansion of fluid within the jar. 8. The drilling jar of claim 1 further comprising a mechanical jar module coupled at one end of the outer tubular housing to provide selective operation of either: a hydraulic jar via movement of the valve assembly and oil flow between the first and second oil chambers; or a mechanical jar via the coupled module. 9. The drilling jar of claim 8 wherein the mechanical jar module comprises a compressed spring stack. 10. The drilling jar of claim 9 wherein the mandrel of the inner tubular assembly is configured to deliver an impact blow to the internal anvil when a compressional or tensional force is applied exceeding the force of the compressed spring stack. 11. A method of imparting an impact blow on a drill string disposed in a wellbore, comprising: disposing a drilling jar in a drill string, the jar including: an outer tubular housing having an inner bore; an inner tubular assembly telescopically received within the bore of outer tubular housing; a valve assembly comprising a first valve linked to a second valve, the assembly disposed in an annulus between the inner tubular assembly and the outer tubular housing; the valve assembly disposed in between a first oil chamber and a second oil 

 chamber, wherein in response to an axial force applied to the inner tubular assembly or the outer tubular housing the valve assembly is configured to travel longitudinally within the annulus to meter fluid communication between the first oil chamber and the second oil chamber; the inner tubular assembly comprising a housing having longitudinal spline ridge cuts on an interior surface thereof; a mandrel having spline ridge protrusions on an exterior surface thereof; the mandrel disposed within the inner tubular assembly housing with the spline ridge protrusions matingly engaged with the housing spline ridge cuts; and at least one of the spline ridge protrusions on the mandrel having an angled surface to provide a flow channel for the oil when the spline ridge protrusion is matingly engaged with a housing spline ridge cut. 12. The method of claim 11 wherein the first oil chamber, the second oil chamber, and the flow channel defined by the spline ridge protrusion engaged with the housing spline ridge cut are linked to provide an internal fluid passageway to allow oil flow internally along the length of the jar. 13. The method of claim 12 wherein the valve assembly is configured to travel longitudinally within the annulus through a radially-narrowed section in the annulus. 14. The method of claim 13 wherein upon passage of the first valve or the second valve through the radially-narrowed annulus section oil flow between the first oil chamber and the second oil chamber is increased. 15. The method of claim 14 wherein a mandrel of the inner tubular assembly is configured to deliver an impact blow to an internal anvil when the oil flow between the first oil chamber and the second oil chamber is increased. 16. The method of claim 15 wherein the mandrel having the spline ridge protrusions is configured to telescopically slide within the inner tubular assembly housing, with the oil flow facilitated internally along the housing via the flow channel. 17. The method of claim 11 wherein a piston assembly is disposed in the jar to accommodate thermal expansion of fluid within the jar. 18. The method of claim 11 further comprising coupling a mechanical jar module at one end of the outer tubular housing to provide selective operation of either: a hydraulic jar via movement of the valve assembly and oil flow between the first and second oil chambers; or a mechanical jar via the coupled module. 19. The method of claim 18 wherein the mechanical jar module comprises a compressed spring stack. 20. The method of claim 19 wherein the mandrel of the inner tubular assembly is configured to deliver an impact blow to the internal anvil when a compressional or tensional force is applied exceeding the force of the compressed spring stack.
